paraphrase: Belarus competed in the 2014 Eurovision Song Contest, submitting the track "Cheesecake," which was co-authored by Dmitry Novik and Yuriy Vashchuk. The performer of this song was Teo, the stage name utilized by the vocalist Yuriy Vashchuk. The Belarusian entry for the 2014 competition, held in Copenhagen, Denmark, was chosen via a national selection event orchestrated by the National State Television and Radio Company of the Republic of Belarus (BTRC), the country's national broadcaster. This national final comprised fourteen different acts vying for the opportunity, participating in a televised program where Teo's rendition of "Cheesecake" ultimately emerged victorious. The winner was determined by combining the scores from an expert jury panel and the results of a public televote. Belarus was assigned a slot to contend in the second semi-final of the Eurovision Song Contest, an event that occurred on May 8, 2014. During this broadcast, the Belarusian representative performed in the show.

The list of nominees was publicly revealed on January 6, 2023. During this 16th annual awards ceremony, a total of 30 cinematic productions originating from 22 distinct regions and nations were selected for a grand total of 81 nominations across various categories. original: Euromaidan (; Ukrainian: Євромайдан, romanized: Yevromaidan, lit. 'Euro Square', IPA: [jeu̯romɐjˈdɑn]), or the Maidan Uprising, was a wave of demonstrations and civil unrest in Ukraine, which began on 21 November 2013 with large protests in Maidan Nezalezhnosti (Independence Square) in Kyiv. The protests were sparked by President Viktor Yanukovych's sudden decision not to sign the European Union–Ukraine Association Agreement, instead choosing closer ties to Russia and the Eurasian Economic Union. Ukraine's parliament had overwhelmingly approved of finalizing the Agreement with the EU, but Russia had put pressure on Ukraine to reject it. The scope of the protests widened, with calls for the resignation of Yanukovych and the Azarov government. Protesters opposed what they saw as widespread government corruption, abuse of power, human rights violations, and the influence of
paraphrase: Euromaidan (Ukrainian: Євромайдан, romanized: Yevromaidan, literally 'Euro Square', IPA: [jeu̯romɐjˈdɑn]), also referred to as the Maidan Uprising, constituted a series of public demonstrations and periods of civil disorder that occurred across Ukraine. These events commenced on November 21, 2013, initiated by substantial gatherings and public displays of dissent in Kyiv's Maidan Nezalezhnosti, commonly known as Independence Square. The catalyst for these protests was President Viktor Yanukovych's abrupt choice to refrain from endorsing the Association Agreement between the European Union and Ukraine. Instead, he opted to foster stronger connections with Russia and the Eurasian Economic Union. It is noteworthy that Ukraine's legislative body had previously given its strong endorsement for the finalization of this Agreement with the EU; however, Russia had exerted considerable pressure on Ukraine to decline its ratification. As a result, the extent of these demonstrations expanded considerably, leading to demands for the removal of both Yanukovych from his presidential post and the Azarov government. Those participating in the protests voiced their opposition to what they perceived as prevalent governmental corruption, misuse of authority, violations of human rights, and the pervasive influence of various external factors.

original: The 16th Congress of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Saguia El Hamra and Río de Oro (Arabic: المؤتمر السادس عشر للجبهة الشعبية لتحرير الساقية الحمراء ووادي الذهب, Spanish: XVI Congreso del Frente Popular para la Liberación de Saguia El Hamra y Río de Oro) was held in the Dajla refugee camp in Tindouf, Algeria between 13 and 22 January 2023 to renovate the governing bodies of the POLISARIO Front and establish the movement's main lines of action and strategy for the next leadership term. The Congress was also used to elect a new General-Secretary for the POLISARIO Front, which would automatically become the President of the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic. For the first time ever, the candidate for general-secretary was not agreed upon before the Congress.
paraphrase: The 16th assembly of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Saguia El Hamra and Río de Oro (in Arabic: المؤتمر السادس عشر للجبهة الشعبية لتحرير الساقية الحمراء ووادي الذهب, and in Spanish: XVI Congreso del Frente Popular para la Liberación de Saguia El Hamra y Río de Oro) took place in the Dajla refugee camp, situated in Tindouf, Algeria, from January 13 to January 22, 2023. The primary objectives of this gathering were to overhaul the POLISARIO Front's administrative structures and to delineate the movement's core operational principles and strategic direction for the forthcoming leadership period. Furthermore, the Congress served as the platform for selecting a new General-Secretary for the POLISARIO Front. This individual would concurrently assume the role of President of the Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic. Notably, this particular Congress marked the inaugural occasion where the contender for the general-secretary position had not been pre-determined prior to the commencement of the assembly.

original: General elections were held in Antigua and Barbuda on 18 January 2023 to elect members of the House of Representatives. The Labour Party (ABLP) has held an absolute majority of 15 seats in the House of Representatives after the 2018 general election, with Gaston Browne remaining as prime minister. Browne initiated a constitutional referendum after the 2018 election, which was rejected by voters, and following the death of Elizabeth II in 2022, he announced his intention to organise a referendum for the transition of Antigua and Barbuda to a republican system. paraphrase: On January 18, 2023, general elections took place in Antigua and Barbuda with the purpose of choosing the representatives for the House of Representatives. Following the general election in 2018, the Labour Party (ABLP) had secured a clear majority, holding 15 seats within the House of Representatives, and Gaston Browne continued in his role as prime minister. Subsequent to the 2018 election, Browne put forward a constitutional referendum, which was ultimately voted down by the populace. Furthermore, after the passing of Elizabeth II in 2022, he declared his plan to arrange a referendum concerning Antigua and Barbuda's shift to a republican form of government. For the 2023 general election, in addition to the ABLP, candidate nominations were submitted by the United Progressive Party (UPP), the Democratic National Alliance, the Barbuda People's Movement (BPM), and three individual politicians running independently. Throughout the period of the election campaign, the UPP put forth a proposal to increase the minimum wage, among other pledges.

original: The 76th British Academy Film Awards, more commonly known as the BAFTAs, were held on 19 February 2023, honouring the best national and foreign films of 2022, at the Royal Festival Hall within London's Southbank Centre. Presented by the British Academy of Film and Television Arts, accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2022. The nominations were announced on 19 January 2023 via a global livestream, hosted by actors Hayley Atwell and Toheeb Jimoh, from the arts charity's newly redeveloped HQ at 195 Piccadilly, London. The EE Rising Star Award nominees, which is the only category voted for by the British public, were announced on 17 January 2023.The German-language epic anti-war drama All Quiet
paraphrase: The 76th annual British Academy Film Awards ceremony, often referred to as the BAFTAs, took place on February 19, 2023, to celebrate the most outstanding cinematic achievements, both domestically and internationally, from the year 2022. This prestigious event was hosted at the Royal Festival Hall, situated within the Southbank Centre in London. The British Academy of Film and Television Arts was responsible for presenting the awards, which recognized exceptional full-length films and documentaries of any origin that were screened in British movie theaters during 2022. The list of nominees was unveiled on January 19, 2023, during a worldwide live broadcast led by actors Hayley Atwell and Toheeb Jimoh, originating from the recently renovated headquarters of the arts charity at 195 Piccadilly, London. The nominees for the EE Rising Star Award, which represents the sole category determined by public vote in the UK, had been revealed two days prior, on January 17, 2023. The German-language epic anti-war drama, *All Quiet*.

original: Opération Chammal is a French military operation in Iraq and Syria launched to help curtail the expansion of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and to support the Iraqi Army. Its name comes from the Shamal (Chammal in French), a northwesterly wind that blows over Iraq and the Persian Gulf states.Airstrikes over Iraq started 19 September 2014 and airstrikes over Syria started by the end of September 2015. The French operation was at first limited to airstrikes and French president François Hollande had stated that no ground troops would be deployed in the conflict. paraphrase: Operation Chammal is a military undertaking initiated by France in the regions of Iraq and Syria. Its primary objectives are to assist in curbing the growth of the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and to provide aid to the Iraqi Army. The operation's designation is derived from the Shamal, which is a northwesterly wind that sweeps across Iraq and the nations bordering the Persian Gulf, and is spelled "Chammal" in French. Aerial bombardments commenced in Iraq on September 19, 2014, while similar air attacks in Syria began in late September 2015. Initially, the French involvement was restricted to these aerial assaults, with French president François Hollande explicitly declaring that no ground forces would be dispatched to the conflict zone. Furthermore, the French frigate Jean Bart was integrated into the United States Navy's Commander Task Force 50 (CTF 50), serving in an escort capacity. On November 14, 2015, the terrorist organization ISIS asserted responsibility for the terrorist acts that occurred.

original: The 28th Critics' Choice Awards were presented on January 15, 2023, at the Fairmont Century Plaza Hotel in Los Angeles, California, honoring the finest achievements of filmmaking and television programming in 2022. The ceremony was broadcast on The CW and hosted by Chelsea Handler, taking over the reins from Taye Diggs who had hosted the show consecutively in the previous four years.Like in the previous two years, film and television nominations were announced separately. The television nominations were announced on December 6, 2022. original: The Colombian peace process is the peace process between the Colombian government of President Juan Manuel Santos and the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC–EP) to bring an end to the Colombian conflict, which eventually led to the Peace Agreements between the Colombian Government of Juan Manuel Santos and FARC-EP. Negotiations began in September 2012, and mainly took place in Havana, Cuba. Negotiators announced a final agreement to end the conflict and build a lasting peace on August 24, 2016. However, a referendum to ratify the deal on October 2, 2016 was unsuccessful after 50.2% of voters voted against the agreement with 49.8% voting in favor. Afterward, the Colombian government and the FARC signed a revised peace deal on November 24 and sent it to Congress for ratification
paraphrase: The peace process in Colombia involved the Colombian government, under President Juan Manuel Santos, and the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC–EP), with the objective of concluding the prolonged Colombian conflict. This initiative ultimately culminated in the formal Peace Agreements established between the Colombian Government, led by Juan Manuel Santos, and the FARC-EP. Discussions commenced in September 2012 and were predominantly conducted in Havana, Cuba. On August 24, 2016, the negotiating parties declared that they had reached a definitive agreement designed to terminate the conflict and establish an enduring peace. Nevertheless, a national referendum held on October 2, 2016, which aimed to officially approve the accord, did not succeed; 50.2% of the electorate cast their votes in opposition to the agreement, while 49.8% supported it. Following this outcome, a modified peace accord was signed by the Colombian government and the FARC on November 24, and subsequently forwarded to Congress for its official endorsement.

original: Chang'e 5-T1 (Chinese: 嫦娥五号T1; pinyin: Cháng'é wǔhào T1) was an experimental robotic spacecraft that was launched to the Moon on 23 October 2014, by the China National Space Administration (CNSA) to conduct atmospheric re-entry tests on the capsule design planned to be used in the Chang'e 5 mission. As part of the Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, Chang'e 5, launched in 2020, was a Moon sample return mission. Like its predecessors, the spacecraft is named after the Chinese Moon goddess Chang'e. The craft consisted of a return vehicle capsule and a service module orbiter.The return capsule of Chang'e 5-T1, named Xiaofei (Chinese: 小飞), meaning "little flyer" in Chinese, landed in Siziwang Banner, Inner Mongolia, on 31 October 2014, 22:42 UTC. The CE-5-T1 Service Module entered lunar orbit on 13
paraphrase: Chang'e 5-T1, known in Chinese as 嫦娥五号T1 and transliterated as Cháng'é wǔhào T1, constituted an uncrewed, experimental space vehicle. This craft was dispatched to the Moon on October 23, 2014, under the direction of the China National Space Administration (CNSA). Its primary objective was to carry out trials concerning atmospheric re-entry for the capsule design slated for utilization in the subsequent Chang'e 5 mission. The Chang'e 5 mission, part of the broader Chinese Lunar Exploration Program, was launched in the year 2020 and aimed to retrieve samples from the Moon. In keeping with previous missions, this spacecraft bears the name of Chang'e, the revered Chinese goddess of the Moon. The craft itself was composed of two main components: a return vehicle capsule and a service module designed for orbital operations. The return capsule of Chang'e 5-T1, which was christened Xiaofei (小飞 in Chinese), translating to "little flyer," successfully touched down in Siziwang Banner, located in Inner Mongolia, on October 31, 2014, at 22:42 UTC. The service module of CE-5-T1 achieved lunar orbit on the 13th of the month.

paraphrase: During February and March of 2023, South Africa experienced extensive flooding, primarily due to significant precipitation linked to the La Niña weather pattern. This event impacted seven distinct provinces, specifically the Eastern Cape, Northern Cape, Mpumalanga, KwaZulu-Natal, and North West regions. The deluges resulted in a tragic loss of life and inflicted considerable harm upon residential properties, commercial establishments, fundamental infrastructure, roadways, and bridges, while also adversely affecting agricultural crops and livestock populations. A minimum of twelve individuals perished across the various provinces, with additional persons reported as missing. Furthermore, two more deaths were documented within the King Sabata Dalindyebo Local Municipality, located in the Eastern Cape Province, and another fatality occurred in Komani Town, previously known as Queenstown. Numerous individuals were compelled to evacuate their homes in the Lekwa Local Municipality. Residents residing near the Vaal, Vanderkloof, and Bloemhof dams, situated along the Orange River in the Northern Cape Province, were also advised to evacuate following the intentional release of water.

paraphrase: On October 12, 2014, Bosnia and Herzegovina conducted its general elections. These elections determined the composition of the country's Presidency, along with the national, entity, and cantonal government bodies. The voter participation rate for these elections reached 54.4%. The elections for the House of Representatives were conducted in two separate parts: one specifically for the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and another for Republika Srpska. During the presidential election, citizens in the Federation once again chose Bosniak Bakir Izetbegović and also selected Croat Dragan Čović. Concurrently, the electorate in Republika Srpska opted for Serb Mladen Ivanić. The Party of Democratic Action secured the most seats in the House of Representatives, obtaining 10 out of the 42 available. Bakir Izetbegović, the Bosniak representative, was the sole member of the three-person Presidency to retain his position, while Mladen Ivanić, the Serb representative, was chosen to be the new member.

paraphrase: During the year 2014, a set of unprecedented diplomatic discussions transpired involving Wang Yu-chi, serving as the Minister for the Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) of the Republic of China (ROC), and Zhang Zhijun, who held the position of Minister for the Taiwan Affairs Office (TAO) of the People's Republic of China (PRC). The initial encounter occurred in Nanjing on February 11, 2014, marking Wang's journey to mainland China. This event represented the inaugural official interaction between the two governing bodies spanning the Taiwan Strait, following the conclusion of the Chinese Civil War in 1949. Wang, accompanied by his delegation, conducted a tour from February 11 to 14, visiting both Nanjing and Shanghai. Subsequently, from June 25 to 28, 2014, Zhang undertook a reciprocal visit to Taiwan, with the objective of re-establishing connections in the wake of the Sunflower Movement.

This event took place at the Dipoli Hall, specifically in the Otakaari 1 Building, which is part of Aalto University in Espoo, Finland. Aalto University played the role of both the host institution and a co-organizer for the ceremony. This ceremony served as the preliminary event leading up to the Aalto University Academic Summit, which bore the title "The Impact of Universities on Economic Growth." During the ceremony, the WCC honored two prominent scientists with awards. The Albert Einstein World Award of Science was bestowed upon Professor Sir Philip Cohen, an internationally recognized enzymologist, in recognition of his four decades of research dedicated to the study of cellular communication. Furthermore, Professor Federico Rosei was the recipient of the José Vasconcelos World Award of Education, an honor granted to him for his outstanding contributions to university teaching. Jussi Nuorteva, who holds the position of Director of the National Archives of Finland and is also a member of the WCC, formally presented the Winners' Book to the award recipients, including Professor Sir Philip.

paraphrase: Marius, a male giraffe residing at Copenhagen Zoo, was born on February 6, 2012, and died on February 9, 2014. Despite being in good health, he was deemed genetically inappropriate for future breeding programs within captivity because his genetic makeup was too prevalent among the captive giraffe population. Consequently, the zoo's administration made the decision to humanely end his life. In spite of numerous proposals to rehome Marius and an online appeal advocating for his survival, he was euthanized on February 9, 2014. Subsequently, his remains were subjected to dissection and a post-mortem examination during a public educational demonstration, after which they were given to the zoo's lions as food. This incident garnered extensive global media attention and elicited reactions from various organizations and individuals, including threats against the zoo's personnel. From the early 1900s, when record-keeping commenced, until February 2014, the date of Marius's death, a total of five giraffes had been euthanized for comparable "conservation" reasons.

original: Severe Tropical Cyclone Gabrielle was a severe tropical cyclone that devastated the North Island of New Zealand and affected parts of Vanuatu and Australia in February 2023. paraphrase: Eleanor Poppy Miranda de Freitas, an English woman born on June 26, 1990, and deceased on April 4, 2014, ended her own life merely three days prior to the scheduled start of her legal proceedings. She was facing charges of perverting the course of justice, stemming from an alleged false accusation of rape. Her passing ignited considerable discussion regarding the potential ramifications of prosecuting individuals accused of fabricating rape claims. Specifically, concerns were raised about whether such prosecutions might discourage genuine rape victims from coming forward and reporting their experiences. Furthermore, a significant part of the debate focused on the appropriateness of pursuing legal action against vulnerable individuals, given that de Freitas had been diagnosed with both bipolar disorder and depression, and a psychiatrist had evaluated her as being at risk of suicide. Despite these concerns, the decision to prosecute de Freitas received endorsement from both the Director of Public Prosecutions and the Attorney General for England and Wales. Separately, the Metropolitan Police Service provided a financial settlement.

paraphrase: The Brazilian Anti-Corruption Act, formally designated as "Law No. 12,846" and frequently referred to as the Clean Company Act (Lei da Empresa Limpa), is a legislative measure implemented in Brazil in 2014. Its primary objective is to combat corrupt activities involving commercial enterprises operating within the nation. This statute outlines civil and administrative sanctions, and it also provides for potential reductions in these penalties for businesses that collaborate with law enforcement agencies through a formal leniency agreement. Such an agreement must be in written form, mutually signed, and accepted by both the business entity and the government. This law specifically targets juridical persons, encompassing corporations and various other organizations, but it does not extend to individuals, whose conduct is regulated by separate legal frameworks. The provisions of this Act have been applied on multiple occasions, leading to the negotiation of leniency agreements that have collectively restored billions of reals to the Brazilian Treasury. A prominent example of this is the agreement reached with Odebrecht S.A., which independently was a significant contributor to these returns.

paraphrase: A motion of no confidence targeting the Spanish government led by Pedro Sánchez was formally introduced by the far-right political party Vox on February 27, 2023. This motion is scheduled for discussion and a subsequent vote within the Congress of Deputies, taking place across March 21 and 22, 2023. This particular motion marks the sixth instance of a no-confidence vote in Spain's history since its transition to a democratic system. Vox initially unveiled its intention to file this motion of no confidence on December 9, 2022. At the time, the party suggested that the motion should be put forward with an independent candidate at the helm, someone possessing governmental experience. Vox articulated its rationale by asserting that Sánchez's proposed alterations to the Criminal Code, specifically concerning the offenses of embezzlement and sedition, amounted to a "self-coup," drawing a parallel to "that of Pedro Castillo in Peru" which occurred just two days prior. The People's Party (PP), an opposition party led by Alberto Núñez Feijóo, publicly declared that its members would abstain from voting against the motion.

The 55th iteration of the Miss Belgium beauty contest, known as Miss Belgium 2023, took place on February 11, 2023. The chosen venue for this prestigious event was the Plopsaland Theater, located in De Panne, Belgium. Due to the unavailability of the current Miss Belgium, Chayenne Van Aarle, the honor of crowning the new queen fell to Miss Belgium 2021, Kedist Deltour. At the culmination of the ceremony, Emilie Vansteenkiste, representing Flemish Brabant, received the coveted title. Consequently, Vansteenkiste is slated to represent Belgium in the Miss World 2023 competition.

The Miss Belgium organization encountered several unfortunate incidents in the week leading up to the pageant. A significant car accident involving the reigning Miss Belgium, Chayenne Van Aarle, occurred just days before the scheduled event. Her recovery necessitated her absence from the ceremony. original: The Global Pact for the Environment project was launched in 2017 by a network of experts known as the "International Group of Experts for the Pact" (IGEP). paraphrase: The "International Group of Experts for the Pact" (IGEP), a collective of specialists, initiated the Global Pact for the Environment project in 2017. This group comprises over one hundred legal professionals specializing in environmental law and is presided over by Laurent Fabius, who previously served as President of COP21. Subsequently, on May 10, 2018, the United Nations General Assembly passed a resolution, designated as Resolution A/72/L.51 of May 10, 2018, and titled "Towards a Global Pact for the Environment," which set the stage for discussions concerning a Global Pact for the Environment. This resolution garnered 142 votes in support, with 5 nations opposing it (the United States, Russia, Syria, Turkey, and the Philippines), and 7 countries choosing to abstain (Saudi Arabia, Belarus, Iran, Malaysia, Nicaragua, Nigeria, and Tajikistan). Furthermore, on May 8, 2020, the United Nations Environment Programme appointed two individuals to serve as co-facilitators for this ongoing endeavor.

original: The Zhengzhou Airport riot occurred on 5–6 February 2014. Passengers at the airport in Zhengzhou, capital of the Chinese province of Henan, angry over lengthy delays due to snowy weather, smashed computers at airline check-in counters and destroyed information kiosks. It has been estimated that as many as 2,000 people took part over two days before police were able to restore order.Some of the angry passengers had been waiting for as long as five days to catch flights back to their jobs in other cities after their annual trips to see family during the Chinese New Year holiday period. They were further frustrated by the lack of information from airline and airport staff while they waited. The mass incident highlighted the difficulties faced by China's commercial airline industry
paraphrase: A disturbance took place at Zhengzhou Airport between February 5th and 6th, 2014. Travelers at the Zhengzhou airport, which serves as the provincial capital of Henan in China, expressed their frustration by damaging computer equipment at airline check-in desks and demolishing information kiosks. This destructive behavior was a result of significant flight delays caused by snowy weather conditions. It is estimated that approximately 2,000 individuals participated in these events over a period of two days before law enforcement authorities successfully re-established control. A number of the disgruntled passengers had endured waits of up to five days to board flights that would return them to their employment in different urban centers. These individuals were journeying back after their yearly visits to family members during the Chinese New Year holiday season. Their exasperation was intensified by the insufficient communication provided by airline and airport personnel throughout their prolonged waiting period. This widespread incident drew attention to the challenges confronting China's commercial aviation sector.

original: Fit for 55 is a package by the European Union designed to reduce the European Union's greenhouse gas emissions by 55% by 2030. The package was proposed in July 2021 by the European Commission. Under an accelerated legislative process, the plans may become law in 2022. paraphrase: The European Union introduced a comprehensive set of proposals known as "Fit for 55," which aims to decrease the European Union's emissions of greenhouse gases by a significant 55% before the year 2030. These proposals were initially put forward by the European Commission in July 2021. There is a possibility that these proposed plans could be enacted into law during 2022, owing to an expedited legislative procedure. Among the various provisions are enhanced provisions for environmentally friendly transportation options, increased development of renewable energy sources, and the establishment of a tariff, specifically named the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism. This tariff will be applied to the emissions associated with high-carbon goods imported from nations that do not possess adequate domestic measures for reducing greenhouse gas emissions. Furthermore, the package suggests broadening the scope of the European Union Emissions Trading System to encompass the transport and heating sectors. When contrasted with the net-zero projection developed by the International Energy Agency, this particular plan incorporates a greater number of strategies to guarantee that energy costs remain manageable for consumers. However, the environmental advocacy group Greenpeace voiced its disapproval of the package, asserting that it is insufficient for effectively preventing global warming.

original: The 2023 Nigerian currency crisis was precipitated by a shortage of cash currency (the naira) and an attempt by the Nigerian government to force citizens to use a newly created government-sponsored central bank digital currency. This led to extensive street protests in mid-February 2023.Central Bank governor Godwin Emefiele initially cited Nigerians hoarding notes in their homes as the reason for the currency redesign. However, he later claimed that it would deter counterfeiters and those paying kidnappers' ransoms, and that it was a step towards a cashless society. He also argued that it would help reduce inflation. Some analysts and politicians have said that a motive of the CBDC introduction and deprecation of the old currency was to curb vote buying, by thwarting politicians who had amassed a stockpile
paraphrase: The 2023 currency crisis in Nigeria was triggered by two main factors: a scarcity of physical cash (the naira) and the Nigerian government's effort to compel its citizens to adopt a new central bank digital currency, which was government-backed. This situation culminated in widespread street demonstrations during the middle of February 2023. Initially, Godwin Emefiele, the governor of the Central Bank, attributed the need for a currency redesign to Nigerians accumulating banknotes in their residences. Nevertheless, he subsequently asserted that this measure would discourage counterfeiters and individuals paying ransoms to kidnappers, simultaneously serving as a move toward a society that relies less on cash. Furthermore, he contended that it would contribute to a decrease in inflation. Certain political figures and financial analysts have suggested that a primary motivation behind the launch of the CBDC and the devaluation of the existing currency was to curtail vote buying, specifically by frustrating politicians who had accumulated substantial reserves of cash.

original: The 2013–2014 Thai political crisis was a period of political instability in Thailand. Anti-government protests took place between November 2013 and May 2014, organised by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political pressure group led by former Democrat Party parliamentary representative (MP) Suthep Thaugsuban. The crisis eventually resulted in the removal of incumbent Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra, a coup d'état, and the establishment of a military junta. The primary aim of the protests was the removal of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra's – the brother of Yingluck – influence on Thai politics and the creation of an unelected "people's council" to oversee reforms of the political system. Protesters viewed Thaksin as corrupt and damaging to Thailand's democracy, although he enjoyed strong support in many areas of Thailand,
paraphrase: Thailand experienced a period of political turmoil known as the 2013–2014 Thai political crisis. Demonstrations against the government were organized by the People's Democratic Reform Committee (PDRC), a political advocacy group headed by Suthep Thaugsuban, who was a former Member of Parliament (MP) for the Democrat Party. These protests occurred from November 2013 through May 2014. Ultimately, this crisis led to the ousting of the then-current Prime Minister, Yingluck Shinawatra, a military coup, and the subsequent formation of a military junta. The main objective behind these protests was to eliminate the influence of former prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra – Yingluck's brother – from Thai political affairs and to establish a "people's council" composed of unelected individuals to supervise the reform of the country's political system. Despite Thaksin enjoying considerable backing in numerous regions of Thailand, the demonstrators perceived him as corrupt and detrimental to the democratic principles of Thailand.

paraphrase: Cold Water Flat, an American musical group, was established in 1990. Its founding members were Paul Janovitz, who handled vocals and guitar, Ted Silva on bass, and Paul Harding on drums. The three individuals formed the band while they were students at the University of Massachusetts Amherst. Paul Janovitz holds the distinction of being the younger sibling of Bill Janovitz, who is recognized as the lead vocalist and primary songwriter for the band Buffalo Tom. Initially, the band commenced performing concerts in the Boston area. However, the group faced a potential dissolution when Janovitz relocated to New York. Their decision to continue was prompted by an offer from the European record label Play It Again Sam, which expressed interest in releasing an album. Not long after, the ensemble disbanded, and Janovitz subsequently joined the band Dragstrip.

original: Gutter oil, trench oil, sewer oil and tainted oil (Chinese: 地沟油 / 地溝油; pinyin: dìgōu yóu, or 餿水油; sōushuǐ yóu) are Chinese slang terms primarily used in China and Taiwan to refer to recycled oil. paraphrase: In China and Taiwan, the terms gutter oil, trench oil, sewer oil, and tainted oil (地沟油 / 地溝油; dìgōu yóu, or 餿水油; sōushuǐ yóu) are colloquial expressions that denote oil which has been recycled. These terms can refer to the unlawful habit of eateries re-employing cooking oil beyond the duration allowed by safety regulations. Furthermore, they can also signify the processing of spoiled yellow grease gathered from various origins, such as deep fryers in restaurants and refuse from slaughterhouses. The application of gutter oil is met with significant disapproval and frequently results in legal action. For instance, vending gutter oil within China can incur prolonged periods of incarceration or a death sentence with a stay of execution. As an illustration, in 2014, Zhu Chuanfeng, a businessman, received the latter penalty for this offense.

paraphrase: Typhoon Hagupit, pronounced hə-goo-PEET and originating from the Filipino word [hɐguˈpit] meaning "lashing or flogging," received the designation Typhoon Ruby within the Philippines. This meteorological event stood as the second most powerful tropical cyclone observed throughout 2014. During its progression in early December, Hagupit specifically affected the Philippines, gradually diminishing in strength. The typhoon was responsible for the fatalities of 18 individuals and inflicted an estimated $114 million (in 2014 US dollars) in damages across the nation. Before its arrival on land, Hagupit had been identified as the most significant danger to the Philippines in 2014, although its physical dimensions were considerably smaller when compared to Typhoon Haiyan from 2013. The formation of Hagupit into the 22nd tropical storm of the yearly typhoon season occurred on December 1, subsequently evolving into the eleventh typhoon of that year on the following day. Operating within conditions conducive to its growth, the typhoon experienced a swift intensification process, ultimately achieving its maximum intensity northwest of Palau on December 4, characterized by a well-defined eye. Hagupit experienced a minor reduction in strength, but it subsequently regained power on December 5.

original: The 2014 unrest in Bosnia and Herzegovina was a series of demonstrations and riots that began in the northern town of Tuzla on 4 February 2014 but quickly spread to multiple cities in Bosnia and Herzegovina, including Sarajevo, Zenica, Mostar, Jajce, and Brčko, among others, for social reasons and with the aim of overthrowing the government. The riots were the most violent scenes the country had seen since the end of the Bosnian War in 1995. paraphrase: The 2014 civil disturbances in Bosnia and Herzegovina comprised a sequence of protests and violent outbreaks. These events commenced in the northern municipality of Tuzla on February 4, 2014, yet rapidly expanded to numerous urban centers across Bosnia and Herzegovina. Among these locations were Sarajevo, Zenica, Mostar, Jajce, and Brčko. The underlying causes for these disturbances were social in nature, and their objective was to bring about the downfall of the existing government. These violent episodes represented the most intense civil disorder the nation had experienced since the conclusion of the Bosnian War in 1995. The majority of the rioting occurred within the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina entity; the Republika Srpska did not witness a comparable degree of civil unrest or active dissent. Certain media outlets, specifically the BBC and The New York Times, referred to these events as the Bosnian Spring, a designation adopted from other similar movements.

original: The Estación Polar Científica Conjunta Glaciar Unión (or Union Glacier Joint Scientific Polar Station) or Union Glacier Station is a summer Antarctic base of Chile, situated on Union Glacier in the Ellsworth Mountains. It is jointly operated from November to January by the Chilean Antarctic Institute and the three armed forces of Chile. It was inaugurated on 4 January 2014 by Chilean President Sebastián Piñera Echeñique.In 2013, the Antarctic stations Teniente Arturo Parodi Alister and Antonio Huneeus were dismantled and their equipment was transferred to Union Glacier Station. This followed the relocation in November 2010 of the operations of the private American company Antarctic Logistics & Expeditions LLC, which has operated the landing strip on Union Glacier since December 2008 and also runs Union Glacier Camp.After the American
paraphrase: The Union Glacier Joint Scientific Polar Station, also known as the Estación Polar Científica Conjunta Glaciar Unión or simply Union Glacier Station, is a seasonal Chilean research outpost located in Antarctica. Positioned within the Ellsworth Mountains on the Union Glacier, this facility is collaboratively managed by the Chilean Antarctic Institute and the three branches of Chile's armed forces. Its operational period extends from November through January each year. The station was officially opened on January 4, 2014, with Chilean President Sebastián Piñera Echeñique presiding over the inauguration ceremony. Preceding this establishment, in 2013, two other Antarctic stations, Teniente Arturo Parodi Alister and Antonio Huneeus, were disassembled, and their respective equipment was moved to the Union Glacier Station. This consolidation effort came after the private American company, Antarctic Logistics & Expeditions LLC, moved its operational base to the same area in November 2010. Antarctic Logistics & Expeditions LLC has been responsible for the management of the Union Glacier landing strip since December 2008 and also operates the Union Glacier Camp.

original: The Argentine debt restructuring is a process of debt restructuring by Argentina that began on January 14, 2005, and allowed it to resume payment on 76% of the US$82 billion in sovereign bonds that defaulted in 2001 at the depth of the worst economic crisis in the nation's history. A second debt restructuring in 2010 brought the percentage of bonds under some form of repayment to 93%, though ongoing disputes with holdouts remained. Bondholders who participated in the restructuring settled for repayments of around 30% of face value and deferred payment terms, and began to be paid punctually; the value of their nearly worthless bonds also began to rise. The remaining 7% of bondholders were later repaid 25% less then they were demanding, after centre-right and US-aligned leader
paraphrase: Commencing on January 14, 2005, Argentina initiated a process of debt restructuring. This endeavor enabled the nation to restart payments on 76% of its US$82 billion in sovereign bonds, which had entered into default during 2001, coinciding with the most severe economic crisis the country had ever experienced. A subsequent debt restructuring effort in 2010 further increased the proportion of bonds under some type of repayment plan to 93%, notwithstanding persistent disagreements with holdout creditors. Bondholders who took part in the restructuring agreement accepted repayments amounting to approximately 30% of the bonds' original face value, along with deferred payment conditions, and subsequently began receiving timely payments. Concurrently, the market value of their previously almost worthless bonds started to appreciate. The outstanding 7% of bondholders were eventually compensated 25% less than their original demands, following the intervention of a center-right, US-aligned leader.

Though often listed as a Christian organisation, many of its teachings contradict mainstream Christianity. paraphrase: Good News International Ministries (GNIM), recognized more broadly as the Malindi cult and formerly known as the Servant P. N. Mackenzie Ministries, operates as a new religious movement. Its origins trace back to 2003 when Paul Nthenge Mackenzie and his spouse established it in Shakahola, Kilifi, Kenya. While frequently categorized as a Christian organization, a significant portion of its doctrines diverge from conventional Christian beliefs. The group garnered global notice in April 2023 following disclosures that Mackenzie purportedly directed followers to engage in mass self-starvation with the aim of "meeting Jesus," a directive that ultimately led to the fatalities of at least 110 individuals. This organization, frequently characterized as a cult, holds strong anti-Western views, rejecting modern conveniences like healthcare, education, and athletic activities as "evils of western life." Furthermore, Mackenzie has vocally denounced the United States and the United Nations.

original: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17 (MH17/MAS17) was a scheduled passenger flight from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur that was shot down by Russian-controlled forces on 17 July 2014, while flying over eastern Ukraine. All 283 passengers and 15 crew were killed. Contact with the aircraft, a Boeing 777-200ER, was lost when it was about 50 km (31 mi) from the Ukraine–Russia border, and wreckage from the aircraft fell near Hrabove in Donetsk Oblast, Ukraine, 40 km (25 mi) from the border. The shoot-down occurred during the war in Donbas over territory controlled by Russian separatist forces.The responsibility for investigation was delegated to the Dutch Safety Board (DSB) and the Dutch-led joint investigation team (JIT), who in 2016 reported that the airliner had been downed by a Buk surface-to-air missile launched
paraphrase: Malaysia Airlines Flight 17, also identified as MH17 or MAS17, was a commercial flight regularly scheduled to transport passengers from Amsterdam to Kuala Lumpur. On July 17, 2014, this aircraft was brought down by Russian-controlled military units as it traversed the airspace above eastern Ukraine. The incident resulted in the fatalities of all individuals aboard, comprising 283 passengers and a crew of 15. The Boeing 777-200ER aircraft ceased communication when it was approximately 50 kilometers (31 miles) distant from the boundary separating Ukraine and Russia. Debris from the downed airplane was discovered near Hrabove in Ukraine's Donetsk Oblast, situated 40 kilometers (25 miles) from the border. This tragic event transpired amidst the conflict in Donbas, specifically over an area under the control of Russian separatist factions. The task of investigating this incident was assigned to the Dutch Safety Board (DSB) and a joint investigation team (JIT) spearheaded by the Dutch. In 2016, their findings indicated that the airliner was struck by a Buk surface-to-air missile that had been launched.

original: 2014 Kalbajar incident refers to the crossing of a small group of Azerbaijanis into Kalbajar district, their subsequent capture and conviction for murder and sabotage by the Republic of Artsakh. In July 2014, three ethnic Azerbaijanis crossed Nagorno-Karabakh Line of Contact in to Kalbajar District, then controlled by breakaway Republic of Artsakh forces. This was followed by disappearance of 17-year-old local Smbat Tsakanyan who was later found dead from gunshot wounds and who was last seen walking with the Azerbaijanis in a video used as evidence in the trial. The Azerbaijanis were detained for "reconnaissance operations in the area on orders from Azerbaijan’s intelligence service," as well as kidnapping and killing of Tsakanyan. One of the Azerbaijanis named Hasanov attacked Major Sargis Abrahamyan and his companion Karine Davtyan
paraphrase: The Kalbajar incident of 2014 involved a small contingent of Azerbaijanis who penetrated the Kalbajar district, leading to their subsequent apprehension and their conviction for offenses of murder and sabotage by the Republic of Artsakh. During July of 2014, three individuals of Azerbaijani ethnicity traversed the Nagorno-Karabakh Line of Contact, entering the Kalbajar District, an area then under the control of forces from the separatist Republic of Artsakh. This occurrence was closely succeeded by the vanishing of Smbat Tsakanyan, a local resident aged 17, whose body was later discovered exhibiting gunshot wounds. Tsakanyan had been last observed in the company of the Azerbaijanis in footage presented as evidence during the legal proceedings. The Azerbaijani individuals were taken into custody on charges related to "reconnaissance operations in the region carried out under directives from Azerbaijan’s intelligence service," in addition to the abduction and homicide of Tsakanyan. One of the Azerbaijanis, identified as Hasanov, assaulted Major Sargis Abrahamyan and his associate Karine Davtyan.

Jerrod Carmichael hosted the ceremony.The nominees were announced on December 12, 2022. paraphrase: The economic challenges currently gripping Pakistan, known as the 2022-2023 Pakistan economic crisis, constitute a component of the broader political instability experienced by the nation during the years 2022 and 2023. For several months, this crisis has resulted in considerable economic hardships, manifesting in elevated costs for essential goods such as food, natural gas, and petroleum. A significant factor contributing to the global surge in fuel prices has been the Russian invasion of Ukraine. Furthermore, a history of substantial external borrowing by Pakistan over numerous years has created the serious risk of a sovereign default, leading to a depreciation of its currency and, consequently, an increase in the relative cost of imported goods. By June 2022, the rate of inflation had reached unprecedented levels, coinciding with a simultaneous increase in food prices. The confluence of ineffective governance and a low per capita productivity, particularly when compared to other developing nations with low to middle incomes, has exacerbated a balance of payments crisis. This situation means that the country is currently incapable of generating sufficient foreign currency to finance its import requirements.

On 21 March 2023, the bill was read a third time, and was then sent to President Yoweri Museveni for assent. Museveni returned the bill to parliament for reconsideration on 21 April 2023.Under the bill, persons convicted of gay sex are liable imprisonment for life. paraphrase: On February 6, 2023, a confrontation took place between the China Coast Guard and the Philippine Coast Guard in the vicinity of the Second Thomas Shoal. This shoal is an integral part of the Spratly Islands, an area that is currently embroiled in a broader territorial disagreement within the South China Sea. Several nations, including both China and the Philippines, assert ownership over the Second Thomas Shoal. The Philippines exercises control over this geographic feature through the deliberate grounding of the BRP Sierra Madre upon the shoal. The Philippine Coast Guard's vessel, the BRP Malapascua, was en route to the shoal to carry out a crew rotation and resupply operation for the BRP Sierra Madre. During this transit, the CCG 5205, a vessel belonging to the China Coast Guard, obstructed the path of the Filipino coast guard ship. The Chinese vessel's action was predicated on its perception of the Philippine vessel's presence as an encroachment, and it subsequently directed a green laser light towards it.

paraphrase: The G7's 40th summit occurred in Brussels, Belgium, from June 4th to 5th, 2014. Initially, this gathering was planned as the "40th G8 summit" and Russia was designated as the host, specifically in the Black Sea resort of Sochi. Nevertheless, on March 24th, the remaining seven nations collectively determined that the summit would instead be conducted in Brussels, excluding Russia. The G8 functions as an informal assembly that gathers the leaders of significant global powers, comprising Germany, France, the United Kingdom, Italy, Japan, the United States, and Canada.

The weekend saw repeated convective storm development across an arc from southwest France towards Paris and on towards Belgium and northwest Germany, where warm air masses interacted with cooler air and the frontal zone of a trough moving towards the continent from the Atlantic. paraphrase: During the 2014 Pentecost weekend, a succession of severe supercell thunderstorms and mesoscale convective systems (MCSs) impacted western and central Europe. These weather events occurred in the wake of a heatwave that had characterized early June 2014, itself a consequence of a synoptic weather pattern known as a Spanish plume. Throughout this specific weekend, recurrent convective storm activity was observed developing along an expansive arc. This arc stretched from the southwestern region of France, extended through Paris, and continued onward into Belgium and finally northwestern Germany. In these areas, warm air masses engaged in an interaction with cooler air and the frontal zone associated with a trough that was migrating from the Atlantic Ocean towards the European continent. The severe weather episodes linked to this extensive system persisted for a duration exceeding five days, specifically from June 6 through June 11. The most significant destruction and impact were recorded in the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia on June 9, where the storm was characterized by local observers as being among the most intense witnessed in several decades.

The list of nominees was unveiled on January 23, 2023. original: The 13N was a Cacerolazo against President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner that took place in Argentina on November 13, 2014. It was organized by social networks. The people in the demonstration protested against the high crime rate, the high inflation and the corruption scandals. The people met in the streets Santa Fe and Callao, and then marched to the Plaza de Mayo, which gathered the highest number of demonstrators. There was a strong security network at the Plaza de Mayo and the Congressional Plaza. The demonstration a lower number of demonstrators than previous ones such as the 8N and the 18A.Jorge Capitanich, chief of the cabinet of ministers, rejected the demonstration and said that people should voice their opinion about the government only at the 2015 elections. ==
paraphrase: The 13N, a Cacerolazo, occurred in Argentina on November 13, 2014, and was directed against President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner. This event was orchestrated through social media platforms. Participants in the protest expressed their disapproval concerning the elevated crime rate, the escalating inflation, and various corruption controversies. The demonstrators initially convened at the intersection of Santa Fe and Callao streets, subsequently proceeding to the Plaza de Mayo, where the most significant concentration of protesters was observed. A robust security apparatus was deployed at both the Plaza de Mayo and the Congressional Plaza. The turnout for this demonstration was comparatively smaller than that of earlier protests, specifically the 8N and the 18A. Jorge Capitanich, who held the position of chief of the cabinet of ministers, dismissed the protest, stating that individuals ought to articulate their views regarding the government exclusively during the 2015 elections.

paraphrase: Hamas, the ruling power in the Gaza Strip, has developed an elaborate system of military tunnels since taking control of the area in 2007. This subterranean network extends beneath numerous Gazan municipalities and urban centers, including Khan Yunis, Jabalia, and the Shati refugee camp. The internal passages, spanning several dozens of kilometers within the confines of the Gaza Strip, serve multiple purposes. Hamas employs these tunnels to clandestinely store its rocket stockpiles below ground, to enable secure communication, to conceal ammunition supplies, and to shield its fighters, thereby complicating aerial surveillance and detection efforts. original: Conference of Western Balkan States was a 28 August 2014 conference of heads of states and governments of Western Balkans region initiated by German chancellor Angela Merkel. An idea for organization of conference came in light of the celebration of the 100th anniversary of the beginning of World War I. It is the first conference in this framework and organizers expressed their desire to continue it in future with Vienna and Paris mentioned as a potential next hosts after Berlin (so called Berlin Process). Günther Oettinger confirmed at conference that event will be organised annually with Vienna as a host city in 2015 and Paris in 2016.The German chancellor announced intention to organize a conference on 7 June 2014. Invitation for participation was sent to all the countries
paraphrase: The gathering of Western Balkan nations, which took place on August 28, 2014, brought together the leaders and prime ministers from the Western Balkans area. This event was spearheaded by German Chancellor Angela Merkel. The concept for arranging this conference emerged from the commemoration of the centennial of the outbreak of World War I. This particular conference marked the inaugural meeting within this established framework, and its organizers articulated their aspiration for its continuation in subsequent years, with Vienna and Paris being cited as prospective future host cities following Berlin (an initiative colloquially known as the Berlin Process). Günther Oettinger affirmed during the conference that this event would be held annually, with Vienna scheduled to host in 2015 and Paris in 2016. The German Chancellor had previously declared her intention to arrange such a conference on June 7, 2014. Invitations to participate were extended to all relevant countries.

original: In the afternoon of January 21, 2014, the Chinese internet suffered a major failure. The country's DNS infrastructure, which is responsible for translating domain names into IP addresses, started directing unrelated domains from various TLDs to the completely unresponsive IP address 65.49.2.178 at 15:10 (UTC+8). As a result, two-thirds of all domestic websites became non-functional, including such high-traffic sites as Baidu and Sina.It is debated what caused this incident. Chinese officials point to the fact that the IP address is owned by Dynamic Internet Technology, an American Falun Gong-affiliated corporation most known for developing the Great Firewall circumvention tool Freegate, and argue that it was caused by external hacking. Independent researchers, however, argue that the incident is more likely caused by a misconfiguration in Great Firewall's DNS poisoning
paraphrase:
On the afternoon of January 21, 2014, a significant outage impacted the Chinese internet. The nation's DNS infrastructure, which plays a crucial role in converting domain names into their corresponding IP addresses, began rerouting diverse, unconnected domains from numerous top-level domains (TLDs) to the entirely unresponsive IP address 65.49.2.178. This redirection commenced at 15:10 (UTC+8). Consequently, approximately two-thirds of all websites hosted within the country ceased to function, including widely visited platforms such as Baidu and Sina.

The precise origin of this incident remains a subject of contention. Chinese authorities have highlighted that the IP address in question belongs to Dynamic Internet Technology, an American company associated with Falun Gong and primarily recognized for its development of Freegate, a tool designed to bypass the Great Firewall. Based on this, they contend that the outage was a consequence of external hacking activities. Conversely, independent investigators suggest that the incident was more probably the result of a misconfiguration within the Great Firewall's DNS poisoning system.

original: World news or international news or even foreign coverage is the news media jargon for news from abroad, about a country or a global subject. For journalism, it is a branch that deals with news either sent by foreign correspondents or news agencies, or – more recently – information that is gathered or researched through distance communication technologies, such as telephone, satellite TV or the internet. Although in most of the English-speaking world this field is not usually regarded as a specific specialization for journalists, it is so in nearly all the world. Particularly in the United States, there is a blurred distinction between world news and "national" news when they include directly the national government or national institutions, such as wars in which the US are involved
paraphrase: Global news, also known as international news or overseas reporting, refers to the term used in media to describe news originating from outside a country's borders, focusing on another nation or a worldwide topic. Within the realm of journalism, this constitutes a particular sector that handles reports either provided by journalists stationed abroad or by news organizations. Increasingly, it also encompasses data collected or investigated using remote communication tools, including but not limited to telephones, satellite television, or the internet. While in most English-speaking regions this area typically isn't considered a distinct expertise for journalists, it is indeed recognized as such almost everywhere else globally. Especially in the United States, the boundary between world news and "national" news becomes unclear when the reports directly involve the national government or national organizations, for instance, in conflicts where the U.S. is participating.

original: The 2023 Berlin repeat state election was held on 12 February 2023 to once again elect the 19th Abgeordnetenhaus of Berlin. The 19th Abgeordnetenhaus was originally elected on 26 September 2021. On 16 November 2022, the Constitutional Court of the State of Berlin declared the results invalid due to numerous irregularities and ordered a repeat election within 90 days. A decision by the Federal Constitutional Court regarding five constitutional complaints is still pending, but will not be decided until after the repeat election. paraphrase: Banksy's artwork, titled Spy Booth, was located in Cheltenham, England. Many interpreted the piece as a commentary on the worldwide surveillance revelations that came to light in 2013. In 2014, Robin Barton and Bankrobber London played a role in the artwork's conservation efforts and also tried to facilitate its detachment and subsequent sale. Nevertheless, because the artwork was situated on a Grade II listed structure (specifically, 153–159 Fairview Road), the local authorities intervened, preventing its removal. They granted retrospective listed building consent in 2015, which offered a degree of protection against its dismantling. Regardless of these measures, the artwork was indeed taken down, leading to its destruction in August 2016. Spy Booth was later put up for auction in two forms: as nine fragments of stucco-faced brick that had been cut from the wall, and as a non-fungible token (NFT). The Government Communications Headquarters (GCHQ) has incorporated the image into its materials, utilizing it as a symbolic representation for the question, "How does an analyst?".

original: José Salvador Alvarenga (Spanish: [xoˈse salβaˈðoɾ alβaˈɾeŋɡa]; born c. 1975) is a Salvadoran fisherman and author who was found on January 30, 2014, aged 36 or 37, on the Marshall Islands after spending 14 months adrift in a fishing boat in the Pacific Ocean beginning on November 17, 2012. He survived mainly on a diet of raw fish, turtles, small birds, sharks and rainwater. He swam to shore at Tile Islet, a small island that is part of Ebon Atoll, on January 30. Two locals, Emi Libokmeto and Russel Laikidrik, found him naked, clutching a knife and shouting in Spanish. He was treated in a hospital in Majuro before flying to his family home in El Salvador on February 10.Alvarenga's story was heavily reported worldwide despite initial criticism

Even though convection was wrapping into a curved band pattern, the system organized into a tropical depression on January 18th. original: Presidential elections were held in Afghanistan on 5 April 2014, with a second round held on 14 June. Incumbent President Hamid Karzai was not eligible to run due to term limits. The registration period for presidential nominations was open from 16 September 2013 until 6 October 2013. A total of 27 candidates were confirmed to be running for office. However, on 22 October Afghanistan's Independent Election Commission disqualified 16 of the candidates, leaving only 11 in the race. By April 2014 three candidates gave up the race and decided to support some of the eight remaining candidates. Opinion polls showed Abdullah Abdullah and Ashraf Ghani as the front-runners and indeed the results of the first round of the election had Abdullah in the lead and Ghani behind him.
paraphrase: On April 5, 2014, Afghanistan conducted its presidential elections, followed by a runoff vote on June 14. The sitting president, Hamid Karzai, was precluded from seeking re-election owing to constitutional term restrictions. The window for presidential candidate registration extended from September 16, 2013, to October 6, 2013. Initially, twenty-seven individuals were officially confirmed as contenders for the presidency. Nevertheless, on October 22, the Independent Election Commission of Afghanistan invalidated the candidacies of sixteen individuals, thereby reducing the pool of eligible candidates to just eleven. By April 2014, three of these candidates had withdrawn from the competition, choosing instead to endorse certain candidates among the remaining eight. Public opinion surveys consistently identified Abdullah Abdullah and Ashraf Ghani as the leading contenders, and the initial round of the election confirmed these projections, with Abdullah securing the most votes and Ghani following in second place.

paraphrase: An agreement under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea concerning the conservation and sustainable utilization of marine biological diversity in regions beyond national jurisdiction, often referred to as the Biodiversity Beyond National Jurisdiction treaty, was ratified by an intergovernmental conference at the United Nations on March 4, 2023. This accord, officially known as the United Nations High Seas Treaty, functions as a tool of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS). It encompasses four primary goals: Firstly, it aims to create marine protected zones to safeguard marine biodiversity. Secondly, it seeks to govern the commercial exploitation of marine genetic resources, which possess potential applications in pharmaceutical development, with the purpose of ensuring the fair distribution of their advantages. Thirdly, it strives to ensure fair accessibility to research activities carried out in international waters. Lastly, it endeavors to establish universal guidelines.

original: Action Plan against Disinformation (Czech: Akční plán pro čelení dezinformacím) was a material that was prepared by the government commissioner for media and disinformation Michal Klíma and his team of experts at the government office on behalf of the Cabinet of Petr Fiala. Media learned about the material on December 27, 2022. The strategy proposes possible ways of blocking disinformation, sanctioning its publishers and prosecuting them authors and distributors, but also financial support of "correct media" with tens of millions of crowns per year. The government terminated cooperation with Michal Klíma on February 15, 2023, with immediate effect and abolished the position of government commissioner for media and disinformation, which he had held since the end of March 2022, and transferred the disinformation agenda to the government's national
paraphrase: The document known as the Action Plan against Disinformation (in Czech: Akční plán pro čelení dezinformacím) was created by Michal Klíma, the government commissioner for media and disinformation, and his specialized team situated within the government office. This initiative was undertaken at the behest of the Cabinet led by Petr Fiala. News organizations became aware of this document on December 27, 2022. The outlined strategy put forward potential methods for halting the spread of disinformation, imposing penalties on those who publish it, and pursuing legal action against its creators and disseminators. Furthermore, it suggested providing substantial financial aid, amounting to tens of millions of crowns annually, to "correct media" outlets. On February 15, 2023, the government brought an immediate end to its collaboration with Michal Klíma and simultaneously eliminated the role of government commissioner for media and disinformation, a position he had occupied since the close of March 2022. The responsibilities related to disinformation were subsequently reallocated to the government's national body.

paraphrase: The Ebola virus disease outbreak, which occurred between 2013 and 2016 and was concentrated in Western Africa, represented the largest and most geographically extensive incidence of the illness ever recorded. This event resulted in widespread fatalities and considerable socioeconomic upheaval across the affected area, particularly impacting Guinea, Liberia, and Sierra Leone. Initial instances of the disease were documented in Guinea during December 2013. Subsequently, the contagion disseminated to adjacent nations, Liberia and Sierra Leone, while smaller-scale outbreaks emerged in Nigeria and Mali. Healthcare professionals in both the United States and Spain contracted the virus through secondary transmissions. Furthermore, individual occurrences of the disease were noted in Senegal, the United Kingdom, and Italy. The peak in the number of reported cases was observed in October 2014, after which a steady reduction began, largely attributable to the deployment of considerable international aid and resources. The epidemic was characterized by a substantial death toll and a notably high case fatality rate. By the conclusion of the crisis, the virus had profoundly impacted the region, leaving a lasting legacy.

It was erected on the very spot where the Sveta Petka Tarnovska Cathedral Church previously stood, following the capture of Plovdiv by the Ottoman forces. Subsequently, during the 15th century, specifically throughout the rule of Sultan Murad I, the initial structure was taken down and a new mosque, which is the one present today, was built to replace it. This mosque was known as the Ulu Dzhumaya Mosque, which translates to the Main Friday Mosque. The mosque itself is quite substantial, featuring nine domes and an expansive prayer hall measuring 33 meters by 27 meters (108 feet by 89 feet). paraphrase: The international human rights case, known as Karen Petrosyan v. Azerbaijan, concerned the entitlements of Artush Petrosyan and his offspring, Karen Petrosyan. This ceremony marked the first in-person gathering for the awards in two years, unfolding concurrently at The Beverly Hilton in Los Angeles and the Edison Ballroom in New York. The selection of nominees for the categories encompassing television, short-form projects, and theatre was revealed on October 17, 2022. Subsequently, the nominees for the film categories were made public on January 10, 2023. original: The 2023 Rey de Reyes (Spanish for "2023 King of Kings") was a professional wrestling tournament and supercard event produced by the Mexican Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA) promotion. The event was held on February 5, 2023, at Poliforum Zamna in Merida, Yucatan, Mexico. The 2023 event was the 26th Rey de Reyes show and tournament. In the main event match, Sam Adonis defeated Bandido, Pagano, and Hijo del Vikingo to become the 2023 Rey de Reyes. In other prominent matches, Sexy Star II defeated Chik Tormenta, Lady Shani, La Hiedra, and Dalys in a five-way match to become the 2023 Reina de Reinas and Negro Casas made his AAA in-ring debut, losing to Hijo del Vikingo in a Rey de Reyes qualifier that also involved Myzteziz Jr.
paraphrase: The professional wrestling organization Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA) from Mexico put on the 2023 Rey de Reyes, which translates to "2023 King of Kings." This event encompassed both a professional wrestling tournament and a supercard. The chosen venue for this occasion was Poliforum Zamna, located in Merida, Yucatan, Mexico, and it took place on February 5, 2023. This 2023 iteration marked the twenty-sixth presentation of the Rey de Reyes spectacle and its associated tournament. The climactic encounter of the night saw Sam Adonis emerge victorious over Bandido, Pagano, and Hijo del Vikingo, thereby securing the title of 2023 Rey de Reyes. Among other significant bouts, Sexy Star II bested Chik Tormenta, Lady Shani, La Hiedra, and Dalys in a five-person contest to claim the 2023 Reina de Reinas championship. Furthermore, Negro Casas participated in his inaugural in-ring match for AAA, where he suffered a defeat at the hands of Hijo del Vikingo in a qualifying match for Rey de Reyes, a match which also featured Myzteziz Jr.

original: The 2014 United Nations Security Council election was held on 16 October 2014 during the 69th session of the United Nations General Assembly, held at United Nations Headquarters in New York City. The elections were for five non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council for two-year mandates commencing on 1 January 2015. In accordance with the Security Council's rotation rules, whereby the ten non-permanent UNSC seats rotate among the various regional blocs into which UN member states traditionally divide themselves for voting and representation purposes, the five available seats were allocated as follows: One for Africa One for the Asia-Pacific Group One for Latin America and the Caribbean Two for the Western European and Others GroupThe five members will serve on the Security Council for the 2015–16 period.
paraphrase: On October 16, 2014, the United Nations General Assembly convened its 69th session at the United Nations Headquarters located in New York City, where the 2014 United Nations Security Council election took place. The purpose of this election was to fill five non-permanent positions on the UN Security Council, each carrying a two-year term that would commence on January 1, 2015. Adhering to the established rotation guidelines of the Security Council, which dictate how the ten non-permanent UNSC seats are distributed among the distinct regional blocs that UN member states typically organize themselves into for the purposes of voting and achieving equitable representation, the five vacancies were distributed as follows: one seat was designated for the African region, another for the Asia-Pacific Group, one for the Latin American and Caribbean bloc, and two seats were allotted to the Western European and Others Group. These five newly elected members will fulfill their roles on the Security Council throughout the 2015–16 term.

paraphrase: Operation Full Disclosure, known in Hebrew as מבצע חשיפה מלאה, was a military action undertaken by the Israel Defense Forces in the Red Sea on March 5, 2014. Following an extensive period of observation and reconnaissance conducted far offshore, special forces operatives from the Israeli Navy's Shayetet 13 unit successfully intercepted and boarded the Klos C. This merchant vessel, owned by Iran and registered in Panama, had commenced its voyage from Iran and was en route to Port Sudan, with a planned transit through Iraq. During their search of the ship, the commandos discovered long-range missiles hidden within containers that were ostensibly filled with Iranian bags labeled as Portland cement. These missiles were believed to be intended for delivery to the Gaza Strip. A high-ranking, anonymous Egyptian security official reportedly corroborated that this arms consignment was destined for militant groups operating in either Gaza or the Sinai Peninsula. Subsequently, a panel of experts from the United Nations determined that the weaponry originated in Iran and was being transported to Sudan, thereby leveling an accusation against Iran for its involvement in the illicit transfer.

Incumbent MP Tangata Vavia, of the Democratic Party, received 50 votes, but so did Cook Islands Party challenger Tuakeu Tangatapoto. This resulted in Democratic Party candidate Tangata Vavia being declared elected by 50 votes to
paraphrase:
On November 11, 2014, a special election, known as a by-election, occurred in the Mitiaro electoral district of the Cook Islands. The initial general election in July 2014 for the Mitiaro parliamentary seat had concluded with an equal number of votes for the two main candidates. Tangata Vavia, the sitting Member of Parliament representing the Democratic Party, secured 50 votes. His opponent, Tuakeu Tangatapoto, who was the challenger from the Cook Islands Party, also received 50 votes. Following a recount, the tie was officially verified, which led the High Court to mandate a new election.

Both of the original contenders participated in this by-election. The by-election proceedings were completed, however, the tabulation of votes was postponed due to a formal complaint lodged by the Cook Islands Party. Eventually, the court determined that a single individual had cast an unlawful vote in the Mitiaro district during the general election, and consequently, that person's ballot was deemed invalid. As a direct consequence of this ruling, Tangata Vavia, the candidate for the Democratic Party, was officially pronounced the winner with 50 votes.

paraphrase: Within the field of journalism, the term "local news" denotes the reporting of happenings by news organizations within a specific local setting, which would typically not capture the attention of a different geographic area or possess a broader national or international reach. Local news, differentiating itself from national or international reporting, is tailored to address the concerns of its specific regional and local populations; its primary focus lies on matters and occurrences that are more geographically confined. Several prominent characteristics of local newsrooms encompass regional political developments, meteorological updates, commercial activities, and narratives centered on human interest. A recent study indicates that the audience for local news has experienced a decrease in recent times. Furthermore, with an increasing number of television viewers accessing streamed content, the viewership of local news programs is also starting to diminish. The French Atlantic coastal regions (chiefly Brittany), South West and Southern England, West Wales, Ireland, Spanish Atlantic coastal regions (particularly Galicia) were especially affected by a "conveyor belt" series of high-precipitation storms (mostly not exceptional for their winds) and by high tides. paraphrase: From 2013 to 2014, a sequence of winter storms, originating in the Atlantic, impacted various parts of Atlantic Europe and adjacent territories. Notably, the coastal areas of French Atlantic (with a significant impact on Brittany), the southwestern and southern regions of England, western Wales, Ireland, and the Spanish Atlantic coast (especially Galicia) experienced severe effects. These regions were battered by a continuous succession of storms characterized by substantial precipitation (though their wind speeds were generally not unusually high) and by elevated tides. Numerous storms underwent rapid intensification due to a powerful jet stream, with many deepening to pressures below 950 hPa. The consistent development of extensive, low-pressure systems over the Atlantic generated significant storm surges and large waves. These phenomena coincided with some of the year's highest astronomical tides, resulting in considerable damage to coastal infrastructure. Furthermore, these low-pressure systems delivered intense rainfall, which subsequently caused widespread flooding, reaching its most severe levels across certain areas of southern England.
