original: The 67th British Academy Film Awards, more commonly known as the BAFTAs, were held on 16 February 2014 at the Royal Opera House in London, honouring the best national and foreign films of 2013. The nominations were announced on 8 January 2014 by actor Luke Evans and actress Helen McCrory. Presented by the British Academy of Film and Television Arts, accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2013.Gravity won six of its eleven nominations, including Best Director for Alfonso Cuarón, Outstanding British Film, Best Cinematography, Best Original Music, Best Sound, and Best Special Visual Effects. 12 Years a Slave won Best Film and Best Actor in a Leading Role for Chiwetel Ejiofor. Cate Blanchett
paraphrase: On February 16, 2014, the Royal Opera House in London hosted the 67th British Academy Film Awards, also widely recognized as the BAFTAs. This prestigious event celebrated the most outstanding cinematic achievements, both domestically and internationally, from the year 2013. The nominees for these awards were publicly revealed on January 8, 2014, by the performing artists Luke Evans and Helen McCrory. The British Academy of Film and Television Arts was responsible for organizing the ceremony, during which awards were bestowed upon the finest feature-length motion pictures and documentary films of any national origin that had been exhibited in British movie theaters throughout 2013. The science fiction thriller *Gravity* secured victory in six categories out of its total eleven nominations, which encompassed honors for Alfonso Cuarón as Best Director, Outstanding British Film, Best Cinematography, Best Original Music, Best Sound, and Best Special Visual Effects. Meanwhile, *12 Years a Slave* earned the award for Best Film and also recognized Chiwetel Ejiofor with the award for Best Actor in a Leading Role. Cate Blanchett

original: The Suez Canal Corridor Area Project (Arabic: مشروع تطوير محور قناة السويس) is a megaproject in Egypt that was launched on 5 August 2014 by President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi and completed in 2015. paraphrase: The Suez Canal Corridor Area Project (Arabic: مشروع تطوير محور قناة السويس), a large-scale undertaking in Egypt, was initiated on August 5, 2014, by President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi, reaching its conclusion in 2015. The fundamental objective of this project was to enhance the significance of the Suez Canal region within the global trade landscape and to foster development in the canal cities of Suez, Ismailia, and Port Said. This ambitious endeavor encompassed several key components: the construction of a new urban center in Ismailia, the establishment of an industrial zone, the development of aquaculture facilities for fish farming, initiatives to stimulate growth in the technology sector, the creation of seven new tunnels connecting the Sinai Peninsula with Ismailia and Port Said, the upgrade and modernization of five pre-existing ports, the establishment of novel hubs for logistics and maritime services, and the excavation of a new waterway running alongside the original Suez Canal. The purpose of this newly constructed canal was to augment shipping capacity by enabling simultaneous two-way vessel movement.

original: Art Buff is a graffiti artwork by Banksy which was created in Folkestone in 2014, Banksy announcing it as "part of the Folkestone triennial. Kind of". The work depicts a woman wearing headphones and staring at a plinth, upon which rests a patch of painted-out graffiti. paraphrase: Art Buff, a graffiti piece by Banksy, was produced in Folkestone in 2014. Banksy declared it to be "part of the Folkestone triennial. Kind of." The artwork portrays a woman with headphones on, gazing at a plinth that holds a section of painted-over graffiti. The title of the artwork is a pun, as "buff" is a colloquial term for covering up graffiti with paint. On October 13, 2014, Art Buff was defaced when a penis was spray-painted onto the plinth. In early November 2014, Robin Barton and Bankrobber London arranged for Art Buff to be taken down from its Folkestone site after receiving requests from the wall's owners. They subsequently revealed intentions to offer it for sale, with proceeds benefiting a cancer charity.

This confrontation pitted the government of the Central African Republic's erstwhile Séléka coalition of insurgent factions, predominantly composed of the Muslim minority, against the primarily Christian anti-balaka alliance. original: From January 28 to February 4, 2023, a Chinese-operated high-altitude balloon was spotted in North American airspace, including Alaska, western Canada, and the contiguous United States. On February 4, the U.S. Air Force shot down the balloon over U.S. territorial waters off the coast of South Carolina, on the order of U.S. President Joe Biden. It was shot down by a U.S. F-22 Raptor. Debris from the wreckage was recovered and sent to the FBI Laboratory in Quantico, Virginia, for analysis.The American and Canadian militaries announced that the balloon was for surveillance, while the Chinese government maintained it was a civilian (mainly meteorological) airship that had been blown off course. The U.S. said that the balloon was capable of geolocating electronic communications and carried intelligence surveillance equipment inconsistent
paraphrase: Between January 28 and February 4, 2023, a high-altitude balloon, under Chinese operation, was observed traversing North American airspace. This included regions such as Alaska, the western part of Canada, and the contiguous United States. Subsequently, on February 4, acting on instructions from U.S. President Joe Biden, the U.S. Air Force successfully intercepted and downed the balloon. This incident occurred over American territorial waters, specifically off the coast of South Carolina. The take-down was executed by a U.S. F-22 Raptor aircraft. Following the event, fragments of the downed balloon were collected and subsequently transported to the FBI Laboratory situated in Quantico, Virginia, where they underwent detailed analysis. Both the American and Canadian armed forces asserted that the balloon's purpose was surveillance. In contrast, the Chinese government consistently claimed it was a civilian airship, primarily for meteorological purposes, which had inadvertently deviated from its intended trajectory. The United States further contended that the balloon possessed the capability to pinpoint the geographical location of electronic communications and was equipped with intelligence surveillance technology that was incompatible with its stated civilian function.

original: The Kasta Tomb (Greek: Τύμβος Καστά), also known as the Amphipolis Tomb (Greek: Τάφος της Αμφίπολης), is the largest ancient tumulus (burial mound) ever discovered in Greece, and by comparison dwarfs that of Philip II of Macedon, father of Alexander the Great, in Vergina.It is an ancient Macedonian tomb of the last quarter of the 4th c. BC, and is enclosed in the Kasta mound near the ancient city of Amphipolis, Central Macedonia, in northern Greece. The first excavations at the mound in 1964 led to exposure of the perimeter wall, and further excavations in the 1970s uncovered many other ancient remains. The inner tomb was first discovered in 2012 and entered in August 2014.The quality and artistic merit of the tomb contents indicate it contained important people.
paraphrase: The Kasta Tumulus (Greek: Τύμβος Καστά), alternatively identified as the Amphipolis Burial Mound (Greek: Τάφος της Αμφίπολης), stands as the most expansive ancient burial mound ever brought to light in Greece. Its sheer size significantly surpasses that of Philip II of Macedon, who was the father of Alexander the Great, located in Vergina. This structure represents an ancient Macedonian sepulchre from the concluding quarter of the 4th century BC, encapsulated within the Kasta mound, situated in close proximity to the historical city of Amphipolis, within the region of Central Macedonia in the northern part of Greece. Initial archaeological digs at the mound in 1964 resulted in the unearthing of the surrounding wall, with subsequent excavations throughout the 1970s revealing numerous other ancient artifacts. The internal burial chamber itself was initially uncovered in 2012, and access to it was gained in August of 2014. The exceptional craftsmanship and aesthetic value of the artifacts found within the tomb suggest that it served as the final resting place for individuals of considerable importance.

paraphrase: The fifty-ninth installment of the Eurovision Song Contest, known as the Eurovision Song Contest 2014, was staged in Copenhagen, Denmark. The European Broadcasting Union (EBU) collaborated with the Danish Broadcasting Corporation (DR), serving as the host broadcaster, to organize the event. B&W Hallerne served as the venue for the contest, which was structured with two semi-final rounds on May 6th and May 8th, and a grand final on May 10th, all in 2014. paraphrase: Film Independent presented its 38th annual Spirit Awards on March 4, 2023, a ceremony designed to celebrate the most outstanding independent films and television programs released during 2022. The nominees for the film categories were revealed during a live YouTube broadcast on November 22, 2022, with Raúl Castillo and Taylour Paige making the announcement. Subsequently, on December 13, 2022, Asia Kate Dillon unveiled the nominees for the television categories. Diverging from its historical scheduling, where it typically preceded the Academy Awards by one day, this year's event was held eight days prior to the 95th Academy Awards, which were scheduled for March 12, 2023. This timing positioned the Spirit Awards ceremony squarely within the final voting period for the Oscars, which ran from March 2 to March 7, thus potentially influencing the outcomes of this year's Independent Spirit Awards.

original: ARSAT-1 is a geostationary communications satellite operated by AR-SAT and built by the Argentine company INVAP. ARSAT-1 was launched into orbit on October 16, 2014, from French Guiana alongside Intelsat-30 satellite using an Ariane 5 rocket. It is expected to be located at 72° West longitude geostationary slot. ARSAT-1 is the first geostationary satellite built in Latin America. Total cost of the satellite is 270 million US dollars.ARSAT-1 carries a total of 24 IEEE Ku band (NATO J-band) transponders. Twelve will be operating at 36 MHz, eight at 54 MHz and four at 72 MHz, for a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz. paraphrase: Operated by AR-SAT, ARSAT-1 is a geostationary communications satellite, constructed by the Argentine firm INVAP. Its launch into orbit occurred on October 16, 2014, originating from French Guiana, and it was sent up with the Intelsat-30 satellite via an Ariane 5 rocket. The satellite is projected to be positioned in the geostationary orbital slot at 72° West longitude. Notably, ARSAT-1 holds the distinction of being the first geostationary satellite manufactured in Latin America. The overall expenditure for the satellite amounted to 270 million US dollars. ARSAT-1 is equipped with 24 IEEE Ku band (also known as NATO J-band) transponders. Specifically, twelve of these will operate at a frequency of 36 MHz, eight at 54 MHz, and four at 72 MHz, collectively providing a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz. This satellite is designed to deliver a broad spectrum of services, including telecommunications, data transmission, telephony, and television, primarily serving the entire regions of Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, and Paraguay, with direct broadcast capabilities included.

It is one of 30 intergovernmental agreements the US has concluded with other countries to implement the FATCA.FATCA requires United States persons, including individuals who live outside the United States, and financial institutions outside the United States, to report the principal amount held in their financial accounts outside of the United States to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). paraphrase: The Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA) agreement represents a pact between Canada and the United States, enabling the enactment of this U.S. Congress legislation within Canadian borders. This specific agreement is one of thirty intergovernmental accords that the United States has established with various nations to facilitate the application of FATCA. This legislation mandates that United States persons, encompassing individuals residing beyond U.S. territory, as well as financial organizations situated outside the United States, must declare the primary balance maintained in their financial accounts located outside the United States to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS). As reported by the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation (CBC), approximately one million American citizens currently reside in Canada. This accord additionally impacts their marital partners, offspring, or any individuals with whom they possess shared assets, engage in a business relationship, or jointly maintain an account.

On July 15, China announced that the platform had completed its work and withdrew it fully one month earlier than originally
paraphrase: The confrontation involving the Hai Yang Shi You 981, alternatively recognized as the 2014 China-Vietnam oil rig dispute, describes the heightened friction that developed between China and Vietnam. This friction was triggered when the China National Offshore Oil Corporation, a state-owned Chinese enterprise, repositioned its Hai Yang Shi You 981 oil platform (identified as "Hải Dương - 981" within Vietnam) into waters adjacent to the contested Paracel Islands located in the South China Sea. Subsequent to this action, Vietnamese authorities undertook measures aimed at thwarting the platform's ability to establish a permanent presence. As per a declaration released by China's Hainan Maritime Safety Administration, the drilling operations by the Hai Yang Shi You 981 were scheduled to run from May 2 through August 15, 2014. Nevertheless, on July 15, China declared that the platform had finished its assigned tasks and proceeded to completely remove it a full month ahead of the initial schedule.

original: United Nations Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in the Central African Republic (also called MINUSCA, which is an initialism of its French name Mission multidimensionnelle intégrée des Nations unies pour la stabilisation en Centrafrique) is a UN peacekeeping mission, which started on April 10, 2014, to protect Central African Republic civilians under Chapter VII of the UN Charter. It transformed the 6,000-strong African Union-led peacekeeping force known as MISCA into a UN peacekeeping mission and became operational on September 15, 2014. The UN deployed a transition team to set up MINUSCA and prepare for a seamless transition of authority from MISCA to MINUSCA. As of 30 September 2021, it has more than 15,000 troops, police and civilian personnel on the ground. Its role is to: support for the transition
paraphrase: The United Nations Multidimensional Integrated Stabilization Mission in the Central African Republic, more commonly referred to by its acronym MINUSCA (derived from its French designation, Mission multidimensionnelle intégrée des Nations unies pour la stabilisation en Centrafrique), functions as a peacekeeping initiative of the United Nations. This mission commenced its operations on April 10, 2014, with the primary objective of safeguarding the civilian population of the Central African Republic, in accordance with Chapter VII of the UN Charter. It effectively converted the 6,000-member African Union-led peacekeeping contingent, previously known as MISCA, into a full-fledged UN peacekeeping mission, officially beginning its active duties on September 15, 2014. A dedicated transition team was dispatched by the UN to establish MINUSCA and to facilitate an orderly and uninterrupted transfer of command from MISCA to MINUSCA. Data from September 30, 2021, indicates that the mission currently comprises over 15,000 military personnel, police officers, and civilian staff stationed in the region. The responsibilities of MINUSCA include providing assistance for the ongoing transition.

The match will be played on 3 June 2023 at the Olympiastadion in Berlin.The winner will host the 2023 edition of the DFL-Supercup at the start of the following season, and will face the champion of the 2022–23 edition of the Bundesliga, unless the same team wins the Bundesliga and the DFB-Pokal, completing a double. paraphrase: The championship match of the 2022–23 DFB-Pokal, marking the 80th installment of Germany's yearly football cup tournament, will be determined by the 2023 DFB-Pokal Final. This pivotal game is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2023, at Berlin's Olympiastadion. The victorious team will earn the right to host the 2023 DFL-Supercup at the commencement of the subsequent season. They will compete against the victor of the 2022–23 Bundesliga season, unless the same club secures both the Bundesliga title and the DFB-Pokal, thereby achieving a "double." Should this occur, the team that finished second in the Bundesliga will assume the hosting responsibility and participate in the DFL-Supercup instead. Additionally, the team that triumphs in the DFB-Pokal will secure direct entry into the group stage of the 2023–24 UEFA Europa League. Nevertheless, if the DFB-Pokal winner has already secured a spot in the 2023–24 UEFA Champions League through their league performance, their Europa League group stage berth will be forfeited and reallocated.

paraphrase: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences (AMPAS) organized the 95th Academy Awards, an event that took place on March 12, 2023, at the Dolby Theatre located in Los Angeles. This ceremony served to recognize and celebrate cinematic achievements from films that were released during the year 2022. In the United States, the ABC network broadcasted the entire event, which was brought to life by producers Ricky Kirshner and Glenn Weiss. Furthermore, Glenn Weiss also took on the role of director for the broadcast. The master of ceremonies for the evening was Jimmy Kimmel, a well-known comedian and host of a late-night talk show, marking his third time hosting the prestigious awards. He had previously hosted the 89th and 90th iterations of the ceremony in 2017 and 2018, respectively. The film "Everything Everywhere All at Once" emerged as a dominant force at the ceremony, receiving a leading eleven nominations and ultimately securing seven victories. These wins included significant categories such as Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Original Screenplay, which were awarded to Daniel Kwan and Daniel Scheinert. Additionally, the film garnered three out of the four acting accolades. Other notable recipients of awards included "All Quiet on the Western Front".

original: Patagotitan is a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur from the Cerro Barcino Formation in Chubut Province, Patagonia, Argentina. The genus contains a single species known from at least six young adult individuals, Patagotitan mayorum, which was first announced in 2014 and then validly named in 2017 by José Carballido and colleagues. Preliminary studies and press releases suggested that Patagotitan was the largest known titanosaur and land animal overall, with an estimated length of 37 m (121 ft) and an estimated weight of 69 tonnes (76 short tons). Later research revised the length estimate down to 31 m (102 ft) and weight estimates down to approximately 50–57 tonnes (55–63 short tons), suggesting that Patagotitan was of a similar size to, if not smaller than, its closest relatives Argentinosaurus and
paraphrase: Patagotitan, a genus of titanosaurian sauropod dinosaur, originated from the Cerro Barcino Formation, located within Chubut Province in Patagonia, Argentina. This genus encompasses one species, Patagotitan mayorum, identified from a minimum of six young adult specimens. Its discovery was initially revealed in 2014, and subsequently, José Carballido and his team formally named it in 2017. Initial investigations and public announcements indicated that Patagotitan represented the largest titanosaur and, indeed, the largest terrestrial animal discovered, with projected dimensions of 37 meters (121 feet) in length and an estimated mass of 69 metric tons (76 short tons). However, subsequent studies adjusted the length approximation to 31 meters (102 feet) and reduced the weight estimations to roughly 50–57 metric tons (55–63 short tons). These revisions suggest that Patagotitan’s dimensions were comparable to, or possibly even less than, those of its closest relatives, Argentinosaurus.

original: On 3 October 2014, Canadian Prime Minister Stephen Harper announced that he would put forth a motion to send forces to participate in the coalition for military intervention against ISIL by deploying combat aircraft. On 7 October 2014, the House of Commons approved of sending nine aircraft to join coalition airstrikes against ISIL in Iraq along with 69 special forces advisors to train the Iraqi government's military. The Canadian Armed Forces' contribution to the coalition against ISIL was later dubbed Operation Impact. On 30 March 2015, the House of Commons voted to extend the mission to targets in Syria. No additional forces were announced.In late November 2015, new Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced the withdrawal of Canada's fighter aircraft from the fight against ISIL, but their surveillance and
paraphrase: On October 3, 2014, the Canadian Prime Minister, Stephen Harper, declared his intention to propose a motion advocating for the deployment of troops to engage in the military intervention coalition targeting ISIL, specifically through the use of combat aircraft. Four days later, on October 7, 2014, the House of Commons gave its approval for the dispatch of nine aircraft to integrate into the coalition's aerial assaults against ISIL within Iraq, complemented by 69 special forces advisors tasked with training the Iraqi government's armed forces. This involvement by the Canadian Armed Forces in the anti-ISIL coalition was subsequently named Operation Impact. A vote by the House of Commons on March 30, 2015, resulted in the extension of this mission to include objectives in Syria, though no additional military personnel were declared at that time. Towards the end of November 2015, Justin Trudeau, the newly appointed Prime Minister, revealed plans to pull Canada's fighter jets from the conflict against ISIL; however, the surveillance and refueling aircraft would continue their operations.

This confrontation pits the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) against the Sudanese Armed Forces. Hostilities commenced on April 15, 2023, following claims that the RSF had seized control of Khartoum International Airport, a number of military installations, and the presidential palace, thereby initiating a series of intensifying skirmishes. The combat subsequently extended beyond Khartoum into its surrounding areas.

paraphrase: An office dedicated to the collection or dissemination of news is known as a news bureau. Corresponding terms are employed for particularized bureaus, frequently to denote a geographical area or the extent of reporting: a ‘Tokyo bureau’ designates a specific news organization's facility situated in Tokyo; 'foreign bureau' serves as a general descriptor for a news office established in a nation different from the main operational hub; a ‘Washington bureau’ signifies an establishment, generally positioned in Washington, D.C., which reports on news pertaining to national governmental affairs in the United States. The individual responsible for overseeing a news bureau is commonly referred to as the bureau chief. This terminology is differentiated from a news desk, which pertains to the editorial responsibility of allocating journalists and other personnel, and in other ways organizing, news narratives, and occasionally the actual physical workspace where these activities occur.

original: Between 13 and 18 May 2014 a low-pressure cyclone designated Tamara and Yvette affected a large area of Southeastern and Central Europe, causing floods and landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina suffered the greatest damage, as the rain was the heaviest in 120 years of recorded weather measurements. By 20 May, at least 62 people had died as a result of the flooding, and hundreds of thousands had been forced from their homes. paraphrase: From May 13th to May 18th, 2014, a powerful low-pressure system, named Cyclone Tamara and Yvette, impacted a significant portion of Southeastern and Central Europe, leading to widespread flooding and numerous landslides. Serbia and Bosnia and Herzegovina experienced the most severe consequences, as the precipitation recorded was the highest in 120 years of documented weather observations. By May 20th, a minimum of 62 individuals had lost their lives due to the floods, and hundreds of thousands of residents were displaced from their residences. The municipalities of Obrenovac in Serbia and Doboj in Bosnia and Herzegovina registered the majority of casualties, having been submerged by several-meter-high waters originating from adjacent rivers. The expansive floodwaters triggered more than 2,000 landslides throughout Southeast Europe, extending the destruction to numerous towns and rural communities. The relentless rainfall initiated flash floods and mudslides, consequently affecting several rivers within the Sava and Morava river basins.

paraphrase: The Global War on Terrorism (GWOT), also known as the war on terror, represents a worldwide military effort against terrorism that the United States launched after the September 11 attacks. This campaign stands as the most recent global conflict, encompassing numerous interconnected wars. The primary adversaries in this initiative included militant Islamist and Salafi jihadist armed groups, notably Al-Qaeda, the Islamic State, and their affiliated international networks. These organizations were engaged in military insurgencies with the aim of destabilizing and overthrowing existing governments in several Muslim-majority nations. Furthermore, significant objectives encompassed the Ba'athist regime in Iraq, which was overthrown during an invasion that took place in 2003, along with various militant groups involved in the subsequent Iraqi insurgency. The "war on terror" was formally declared concluded on two separate occasions: in May 2010 and subsequently in May 2013. Additionally, a range of other American military operations were conducted throughout the 2010s.

paraphrase: The monument commemorating the War of 1812, officially designated as Triumph Through Diversity (Triomphe grâce à la diversité in its French title), is a structure crafted from bronze and stone, positioned on Parliament Hill in Ottawa, Ontario, Canada, which serves as Canada's capital city. original: The Cuban thaw (Spanish: Deshielo cubano) was the normalization of Cuba–United States relations that began in December 2014 ending a 54-year stretch of hostility between the nations. In March 2016, Barack Obama became the first U.S. president to visit Cuba since Calvin Coolidge, in 1928.On December 17, 2014, U.S. President Barack Obama and Cuban leader Raúl Castro announced the beginning of the process of normalizing relations between Cuba and the United States. The normalization agreement was secretly negotiated in preceding months, facilitated by Pope Francis and largely hosted by the Government of Canada. Meetings were held in both Canada and Vatican City. The agreement would see the lifting of some U.S. travel restrictions, fewer restrictions on remittances, U.S. banks' access to the Cuban financial system, and the reopening
paraphrase: The process of normalizing the relationship between Cuba and the United States, known as the Cuban thaw (Deshielo cubano in Spanish), commenced in December 2014, thereby concluding 54 years of antagonism between the two countries. Notably, Barack Obama made history in March 2016 by becoming the first U.S. president to visit Cuba since Calvin Coolidge's trip in 1928. The initiation of this rapprochement was publicly declared on December 17, 2014, by U.S. President Barack Obama and Cuban leader Raúl Castro, who announced their intention to normalize relations. This normalization accord was the result of confidential negotiations conducted over several months prior to the announcement. Pope Francis played a facilitative role in these discussions, which were primarily hosted by the Canadian Government. Meetings pertinent to the agreement took place in both Canada and Vatican City. The terms of this agreement included the easing of certain travel restrictions imposed by the U.S., a reduction in limitations on remittances, the granting of access for U.S. banks to the Cuban financial system, and the subsequent reopening.

These cases are poised to determine if the entitlement to marry and form a family should be granted to queer individuals in India. A Constitution Bench comprising five judges, specifically Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud, Justice S.K. Kaul, Justice Ravindra Bhat, Justice Hima Kohli, and Justice P.S. Narasimha, is scheduled to preside over the hearing of 20 interrelated cases initiated by 52 petitioners. The petitioners, who include both queer couples and single queer individuals, are seeking legal affirmation of their right to marry and establish a family unit. This request is founded upon several fundamental rights: protection from discrimination, the right to equal treatment, the right to dignity, personal freedom, the right to privacy, the right to make autonomous personal choices, and the freedom of belief.

paraphrase: The Salvadoran gang crackdown, officially known in El Salvador as the Régimen de Excepción (which translates to State of Exception in English) and the Guerra Contra las Pandillas (which means War Against the Gangs in English), commenced in March 2022. This initiative was launched following a significant surge in criminal activity observed between March 25 and March 27, 2022, during which 87 individuals lost their lives in El Salvador. The Salvadoran government attributed this sharp increase in homicides to the presence and actions of criminal gangs operating within the nation. Consequently, the country's legislative body sanctioned a state of emergency. This emergency declaration led to the suspension of several fundamental rights, including the right to association and the right to legal counsel. Furthermore, it extended the permissible duration of detention without formal charges, alongside implementing other provisions that broadened the authority of law enforcement agencies across the country. By April 12, 2023, a total of 67,203 individuals suspected of having ties to gangs had been apprehended, a situation that has led to significant overcrowding.

original: The 2014 Paris Municipal elections took place on 23 and 30 March 2014, at the same time as other French municipal elections. Anne Hidalgo, representing the Socialist Party, secured the mayoral position for Paris, making her the inaugural female mayor of the city. Prior to this, she served as the city's deputy mayor. Hidalgo achieved victory in the second round, garnering approximately 55% of the total votes. Her primary challenger was Nathalie Kosciusko-Morizet from the UMP, who had actually outperformed Hidalgo in the initial voting round on March 23. original: Operation Car Wash (Portuguese: Operação Lava Jato) was a criminal investigation by the Federal Police of Brazil's Curitiba branch. It began in March 2014 and was initially headed by investigative judge Sérgio Moro, and in 2019 by Judge Luiz Antônio Bonat. It has resulted in more than a thousand warrants of various types. According to the Operation Car Wash task force, investigations implicate administrative members of the state-owned oil company Petrobras, politicians from Brazil's largest parties (including presidents of the Republic), presidents of the Chamber of Deputies and the Federal Senate, state governors, and businessmen from large Brazilian companies. The Federal Police consider it the largest corruption investigation in the country's history. The taskforce was officially disbanded on 1 February 2021.Originally a money laundering investigation, it expanded to
paraphrase: Operação Lava Jato, known in English as Operation Car Wash, was a wide-ranging criminal probe conducted by the Curitiba branch of Brazil's Federal Police. The investigation commenced in March 2014, with Judge Sérgio Moro initially leading the inquiry, and Judge Luiz Antônio Bonat taking over leadership in 2019. Over its duration, this operation led to the issuance of more than a thousand different types of warrants. As reported by the Operation Car Wash task force, the investigations uncovered the involvement of various administrative personnel within the state-owned oil company Petrobras, numerous politicians belonging to Brazil's most prominent political parties (including former presidents of the Republic), presidents of both the Chamber of Deputies and the Federal Senate, several state governors, and business executives from major Brazilian corporations. The Federal Police has characterized this investigation as the most extensive corruption inquiry ever undertaken in the nation's history. The dedicated task force was formally dissolved on the 1st of February, 2021. What started as an investigation into money laundering eventually broadened to encompass a much wider scope of criminal activities.

paraphrase: On Sunday, February 2, 2014, Costa Rica conducted general elections to choose a new president, two vice presidents, and fifty-seven members of the Legislative Assembly.  Due to Article 132 of the constitution, the sitting President, Laura Chinchilla Miranda, was unable to seek re-election for a subsequent term.  The National Liberation Party, which was in power, selected Johnny Araya Monge, the Mayor of San José, as their presidential nominee. The Libertarian Movement party chose Otto Guevara Guth, a former legislator. José María Villalta Florez-Estrada was the nominee for the leftist Broad Front, and the center-left Citizens' Action Party put forward Luis Guillermo Solís Rivera.  Surveys conducted in December 2013 indicated that Araya was leading with 37 percent of the vote, with Villalta closely following at 32 percent. Guevara registered 15 percent, while Solís was in last place with eight percent, hinting at the strong possibility of a second-round election.

original: Parliamentary elections were held in Estonia on 5 March 2023 to elect all 101 members of the Riigikogu. The officially published election data indicate the victory of the Reform Party, which won 37 seats in total, while the Conservative People's Party of Estonia (EKRE) placed second with 17 seats. The Centre Party won 16 seats, a loss of 10, while Estonia 200 won 14 seats, gaining representation in Riigikogu. After the previous parliamentary election in 2019, the Centre Party, led by Jüri Ratas, formed a government with Ratas serving as prime minister. His government was brought down in January 2021 after a corruption investigation, and Kaja Kallas of the Reform Party formed a coalition government with the Centre Party; which collapsed in June 2022. paraphrase: On March 5, 2023, Estonia conducted parliamentary elections to select all 101 members of its parliament, the Riigikogu. The officially released election results confirmed a triumph for the Reform Party, securing a total of 37 seats. The Conservative People's Party of Estonia (EKRE) finished as the runner-up, obtaining 17 seats. The Centre Party acquired 16 seats, representing a decrease of 10 seats compared to the previous election, while Estonia 200 garnered 14 seats, thereby achieving representation in the Riigikogu for the first time. Following the 2019 parliamentary election, the Centre Party, under the leadership of Jüri Ratas, established a government with Ratas as the prime minister. This government dissolved in January 2021 subsequent to a corruption inquiry. Subsequently, Kaja Kallas of the Reform Party established a coalition government alongside the Centre Party; however, this coalition dissolved in June 2022. Kallas proceeded to form a new government.

original: The Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea is the landmark document resulting from the investigations on human rights in North Korea commissioned by the United Nations Human Rights Council in 2013 and concluded in 2014.The report unequivocally concluded that the North Korean government systematically violated human rights including freedom of thought, expression and religion; freedom from discrimination; freedom of movement and residence; and the right to food.The Commission further determined that North Korea had committed crimes against humanity and manifestly failed to uphold its responsibility to protect. paraphrase: The comprehensive document known as the Report of the Commission of Inquiry on Human Rights in the Democratic People's Republic of Korea represents the pivotal outcome of inquiries into human rights conditions in North Korea. These investigations were initiated by the United Nations Human Rights Council in the year 2013 and brought to a close in 2014. This report emphatically determined that the government of North Korea had engaged in systematic breaches of human rights. These violations encompassed, but were not limited to, the freedom of thought, the freedom of expression, and the freedom of religion; liberation from discriminatory practices; the freedom of movement and the freedom of residence; and the fundamental entitlement to sustenance. Furthermore, the Commission ascertained that North Korea was responsible for perpetrating crimes against humanity and had demonstrably neglected its obligation to provide protection to its citizens. These egregious offenses include acts such as "extermination, murder, enslavement, torture, imprisonment, rape, forced abortions and other forms of sexual violence, persecution based on political, religious, racial, and gender affiliations, the involuntary relocation of populations, and enforced disappearance.

The country's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) fell by 3.5% in 2015 and 3.3% in 2016, after which a small economic recovery began. That recovery continued until 2020, when the COVID-19 pandemic began to impact the economy again. The economic crisis occurred alongside a political crisis that resulted in the impeachment of president Dilma Rousseff. These events combined caused mass popular dissatisfaction with the political system. original: Cyclones Judy and Kevin were a pair of intense tropical cyclones that made landfall on the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu within 48 hours of each other in March 2023. They were the fourth and fifth named storms of the 2022–23 South Pacific cyclone season respectively, as well as the second and third severe tropical cyclones of the season. By the end of February, Judy had affected the Solomon Islands, and shortly after, Kevin began to affect the country. The nations were pounded by powerful winds and destructive seas. paraphrase: Tropical Cyclones Judy and Kevin, two powerful tropical cyclones, struck the Pacific island country of Vanuatu in March 2023, making landfall less than two days apart. These cyclones represented the fourth and fifth named systems of the 2022–23 South Pacific cyclone season, and also marked the second and third severe tropical cyclones experienced during that season. Prior to reaching Vanuatu, Judy had impacted the Solomon Islands by the conclusion of February, with Kevin beginning to affect the country shortly thereafter. Both island nations were subjected to considerable damage from forceful winds and turbulent ocean conditions. Vanuatu experienced significant devastation, enduring the impact of both cyclones within a 48-hour period. Following Judy's passage, the government of Vanuatu submitted a request for assistance to Australia and New Zealand. Furthermore, on 3 March, while Kevin was still exerting its force on the islands, a significant earthquake with a magnitude of 6.5 also occurred.
